summary: A stepped pyramid-style defensive structure with 3 tiers, featuring a central watchtower. The building uses wood and stonebrick in horizontal banding patterns, with extensive wooden overhang details on each level. A perimeter fence surrounds the structure on all sides. The interior is hollow with a central courtyard.
rebuild_hints: Build three concentric square platforms, each smaller than the last, creating a stepped pyramid effect
Apply horizontal wood and stonebrick banding on each tier, alternating materials
Add extensive wooden overhang/slab details on the underside of each tier for visual depth
Construct a central watchtower (approximately 4x4 base) rising above the top tier
Place fence posts in a rectangular perimeter around the entire structure
Leave the interior hollow as a courtyard space
Cap the top with dark wood or similar material
Ensure stonebrick reinforces corners and edges of each tier
uncertainties: Exact block count per tier not determinable from angles shown
Interior courtyard details and floor composition unclear
Precise height of watchtower relative to main structure uncertain
Whether fence is decorative or functional barrier unclear
Exact wood type (oak, spruce, etc.) cannot be determined from images
Interior access points and doorways not visible in provided views
